steps.title: Identify the claim
steps.content: The claim is that Newland compares his intellect favorably to that of other men in the audience — he must judge his own mind to be better than theirs.

steps.title: Match the quotation
steps.content:
A directly says Newland felt 'distinctly the superior of these chosen specimens' and had 'read more, thought more' than any of them — exactly a favorable intellectual comparison.
  • B is about reality
  • C admits the men's collective influence on him
  • D describes the audience without comparison
.

That matches choice A — A is correct.
